patch.value:
Repro: 'pm context --json --max-items 5' -> unknown_option; examples only suggest pm context --help.

Why it matters: project management = context management — agents reason in 'max items / top N' terms when bounding token spend, and every failed guess costs a help round-trip. This is the same recovery-hint class as GH-458 (pm-qfte, shipped: lifecycle --assignee alias) and generalizes under pm-gdi7 (unknown-option errors should suggest valid flags). If pm-gdi7's generalized did-you-mean suggestion engine lands first, this item reduces to a targeted test case plus the alias decision.

Duplicate-check evidence: pm search 'GH-476' -> 0 hits; pm search 'context row limit aliases' -> no open coverage; pm-gdi7 covers the generic suggestion engine but not this specific alias/hint.
GH-476: pm context rejects --max-items with an untargeted unknown_option (alias or recovery hint)

Fleet dogfood (GH-476): an agent naturally tried 'pm context --max-items 5' to bound token usage; pm rejects it with a generic unknown_option and no pointer to the supported --limit flag, forcing an extra help round-trip.

Either --max-items is accepted as a hidden alias for --limit on pm context (contracts/completions/docs wired per the new-flag checklist), or the unknown_option error for --max-items returns a targeted hint naming 'pm context --limit <n>'; regression test covers the chosen behavior; GH-476 closed with shipped evidence
